---
title: "dplyr And tidyr Dictionary And Cheatsheet"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

# Goal

This notebook is a compact dictionary of the `dplyr` and `tidyr` functions used in this course.

Use it as a reference when a module asks you to filter rows, select columns, create new variables, summarize data, join data frames, or reshape data.

It is not a complete `dplyr` or `tidyr` reference. For the full references, use the official documentation:

- [`dplyr` reference index](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/)
- [`tidyr` reference index](https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/reference/)
- [`dplyr` cheatsheet](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/html/data-transformation.html)
- [`tidyr` cheatsheet](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/html/tidyr.html)

# Core Structure

| Function or component | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `|>` | Sends the result on the left into the next function. This is the base R pipe, not a `dplyr` function. | [Base R pipe](https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/pipeOp.html) |
| `glimpse()` | Prints a compact view of column names, types, and example values. | [`glimpse()`](https://pillar.r-lib.org/reference/glimpse.html) |
| `head()` | Shows the first rows of an object. This is a base R function, not a `dplyr` function. | [`head()`](https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/utils/html/head.html) |

# Keep Or Remove Rows

| Function | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `filter()` | Keeps rows that match one or more conditions. | [`filter()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/filter.html) |
| `distinct()` | Keeps unique rows or unique combinations of selected columns. | [`distinct()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/distinct.html) |
| `slice_head()` | Keeps the first rows within the current data frame or group. | [`slice()` family](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/slice.html) |
| `slice_max()` | Keeps rows with the largest values of a variable. | [`slice()` family](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/slice.html) |

# Choose Or Rename Columns

| Function | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `select()` | Keeps, drops, or reorders columns. | [`select()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/select.html) |
| `rename()` | Changes column names while keeping the data. | [`rename()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/rename.html) |
| `everything()` | Selects all remaining columns, often after naming a few columns first. | [`everything()`](https://tidyselect.r-lib.org/reference/everything.html) |

# Create Or Change Columns

| Function | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `mutate()` | Adds new columns or changes existing columns. | [`mutate()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/mutate.html) |
| `transmute()` | Creates new columns and keeps only those new columns. | [`transmute()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/transmute.html) |
| `case_when()` | Creates values from multiple conditions. Useful for recoding into categories. | [`case_when()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/case_when.html) |
| `if_else()` | Creates values from one true/false condition. | [`if_else()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/if_else.html) |

# Count Or Summarize

| Function | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `count()` | Counts rows by one or more variables. | [`count()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/count.html) |
| `group_by()` | Defines groups for later summaries or within-group operations. | [`group_by()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/group_by.html) |
| `summarize()` / `summarise()` | Reduces many rows into summary values. | [`summarise()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/summarise.html) |
| `n()` | Counts rows inside `summarize()`, `mutate()`, or `filter()` contexts. | [`context()` helpers](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/context.html) |
| `n_distinct()` | Counts unique values. | [`n_distinct()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/n_distinct.html) |
| `ungroup()` | Removes grouping from a grouped data frame. | [`group_by()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/group_by.html) |

# Sort Rows

| Function | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `arrange()` | Sorts rows. | [`arrange()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/arrange.html) |
| `desc()` | Sorts a variable in descending order, usually inside `arrange()`. | [`desc()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/desc.html) |

# Combine Data Frames

| Function | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `left_join()` | Adds columns from another data frame by matching key columns; keeps all rows from the left data frame. | [`mutate-joins`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/mutate-joins.html) |
| `inner_join()` | Keeps rows with matching keys in both data frames. | [`mutate-joins`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/mutate-joins.html) |
| `right_join()` | Adds columns by matching keys; keeps all rows from the right data frame. | [`mutate-joins`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/mutate-joins.html) |
| `full_join()` | Keeps all rows from both data frames. | [`mutate-joins`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/mutate-joins.html) |
| `bind_rows()` | Stacks data frames on top of each other. | [`bind_rows()`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/bind_rows.html) |

# Reshape Data

| Function | What it is for | Documentation |
|---|---|---|
| `pivot_longer()` | Makes data longer by moving column names into a variable and column values into another variable. | [`pivot_longer()`](https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/reference/pivot_longer.html) |
| `pivot_wider()` | Makes data wider by spreading values across columns. | [`pivot_wider()`](https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/reference/pivot_wider.html) |

# Common Patterns From This Course

## Inspect A Data Frame

Use `glimpse()`:

```r
data |>
  glimpse()
```

## Keep Relevant Columns

Use `select()`:

```r
data |>
  select(id, group, outcome)
```

## Keep Relevant Rows

Use `filter()`:

```r
data |>
  filter(!is.na(outcome))
```

## Create A New Variable

Use `mutate()`:

```r
data |>
  mutate(rate_percent = rate * 100)
```

## Recode Into Categories

Use `mutate()` with `case_when()`:

```r
data |>
  mutate(
    group_label = case_when(
      value < 10 ~ "Low",
      value < 20 ~ "Middle",
      TRUE ~ "High"
    )
  )
```

## Count Categories

Use `count()`:

```r
data |>
  count(group, sort = TRUE)
```

## Summarize By Group

Use `group_by()` with `summarize()`:

```r
data |>
  group_by(group) |>
  summarize(
    mean_value = mean(value),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

## Sort A Summary Table

Use `arrange()` with `desc()`:

```r
summary_data |>
  arrange(desc(mean_value))
```

## Join A Lookup Table

Use `left_join()`:

```r
data |>
  left_join(lookup_table, by = "id")
```

## Make Data Longer

Use `pivot_longer()`:

```r
data |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(measure_a, measure_b),
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  )
```

## Make Data Wider

Use `pivot_wider()`:

```r
data |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = group,
    values_from = value
  )
```

# What To Look Up Next

When you need something not listed here, start with the official [`dplyr` reference index](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/) or [`tidyr` reference index](https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/reference/).

Look up functions by the action you need:

- row operations: `filter()`, `arrange()`, `slice_*()`, `distinct()`
- column operations: `select()`, `rename()`, `mutate()`
- summaries: `count()`, `group_by()`, `summarize()`
- reshaping: `pivot_longer()`, `pivot_wider()`
- combining data: joins and `bind_rows()`
